attempts.insight: `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` elaborates because (i) `open CategoryTheory` resolves the name to `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`; (ii) the `Linear k` enrichment on `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's `Sheaf.linear`; (iii) the iter-014 `HModule'` `noncomputable abbrev` reducibility lets instance synthesis unfold the wrapper to the underlying `Ext` group; (iv) `R = k` is unified automatically. The `≃ₗ[k]` codomain matches because the `Linear k`-enriched abelian category provides `Ext.linearEquiv₀` as a `≃ₗ[k]`. Pattern is identical to iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv`.
